_comment: SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH for every number the site claims about itself.
Phase 2.3. Before this existed, the site claimed anywhere from 100 to 22,000 students
across 82 pages — a 44x spread — plus four founding years. Cross-page consistency is a
named trust signal for AI engines: when a site cannot agree with itself, every number on
it gets discounted, including the true ones.
1. Any new page pulls its numbers from here. Never hand-type a stat into a page.
2. Only put a number here you could defend to a journalist or a regulator.
3. A sub-group can never exceed the whole. No single country, age or course may claim
more students than 'students'. (2,400+ German and 3,100+ 8-year-olds are fine
against 10,000+; they were impossible against the 500+ that was briefly considered.)
4. 'rating' comes from Google Business Profile REVIEWS. It is NOT 'rated by N students'.
Say 'rated 4.9 across 547 Google reviews' — that is the claim you can evidence.

_owner_decisions.students: Owner set 10,000+ on 2026-07-15. Cross-checks: 547 Google reviews against 10,000+ students is a ~5.5% review rate, which is normal; 500+ would imply a >100% review rate. It also matches /about, seo-config.js and the 65 existing on-page claims.
_owner_decisions.founded: 2020 — matches schema.org foundingDate and /about. Pages saying 2019/2021/2022 were drift.
_owner_decisions.reviews: 547 real Google reviews. Do not round, do not inflate, and never restate as a student count.

countries: '15+' is the site majority (35 uses) and is the value above, but NOT swept onto anything — it is not yet evidenced, so it stays a reference value until the owner confirms it.

What IS evidenced: 14 country landing pages exist — Australia, Canada, Germany, India, Ireland, New Zealand, Qatar, Saudi Arabia, Singapore, Sweden, Switzerland, UAE, UK, US. That is 14, which does not reach '15+'.
The site also says 30+ (winter camp x6), 25+ (x3) and 11 (x4). 25+/30+ do not contradict a '15+' floor but nothing evidences them either.

The four '11 countries' uses are STALE, not wrong: screen-time-to-skill.html enumerates 11 by name and is missing exactly Qatar and Saudi Arabia — the Gulf cluster built 2026-07-10, after that page was written. Fixing means re-writing the enumerations by hand, not a sweep.
ASK THE OWNER: how many countries do students actually come from? Then sweep 11/25+/30+ to it and update the enumerated list on screen-time-to-skill.html.
_needs_owner_confirmation.dps_families: 14 DPS pages clone 'Join 500+ families from Delhi's top schools'. Left alone: 500 < 10,000 so it breaks no rule here, but it is an unevidenced subset claim worth confirming.
